title: 3Sum
description:
Given a list of integers `nums`, return every triplet of distinct indices `[a, b, c]` (as the values `nums[a], nums[b], nums[c]`) whose values sum to zero. Each returned triplet's values must be sorted ascending, and no duplicate triplets (by value) may appear in the output. The order of triplets in the result does not matter.

**Example**

```
nums = [-1, 0, 1, 2, -1, -4]  ->  [[-1, -1, 2], [-1, 0, 1]]
```
hints: Sort the array first, then fix one number and two-pointer the rest for a pair that cancels it out.
After sorting, skip over a value if it's the same as the one you just used, at every one of the three positions, to avoid duplicate triplets.

solutions.name: Sort + two pointers
solutions.explanation: Sort nums. For each index i, treat nums[i] as the smallest of the triplet and look for two more values in the remaining sorted suffix that cancel it out, using a left/right two-pointer scan: if the three-way sum is too small, move left up; too big, move right down; exactly zero, record the triplet and advance both pointers. Skipping repeated values at each of the three positions avoids emitting the same triplet twice.
solutions.complexity: Time O(n^2), Space O(1) extra (excluding output/sort)
